title: proc_clean regression
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

`./yosys -p "read_verilog -icells top.v; techmap" -g` fails on master (b5a29460) works fine on yosys-0.8, and from memory, ~2 weeks ago.

```
module mux_case_5_1(i, s, o);
  input [4:0] i;
  output o;
  input [2:0] s;
  \$shiftx  #(
    .A_SIGNED(32'd0),
    .A_WIDTH(32'd1),
    .B_SIGNED(32'd0),
    .B_WIDTH(32'sd0),
    .Y_WIDTH(32'd1)
  ) _34_ (
    .A(i[4]),
    .B(),
    .Y(o)
  );
endmodule
```

## Expected behavior

No ERROR. Even if the result is actually nothing...

## Actual behavior

```
<<SNIP>>
2.4.8. Executing PROC_CLEAN pass (remove empty switches from decision trees).
Removing empty process `$paramod$2c8d16adbbfd7d0174db5fcfe6c817b9d0ad9b77\_90_shift_shiftx.$proc$<techmap.v>:145$45'.
Cleaned up 0 empty switches.
Finding unused cells or wires in module $paramod$2c8d16adbbfd7d0174db5fcfe6c817b9d0ad9b77\_90_shift_shiftx..
  removing unused non-port wire \B.
  removing unused non-port wire $0\i[31:0].
  removing unused non-port wire $0\overflow[0:0].
  removing unused non-port wire $0\buffer[0:0].
Removed 0 unused cells and 4 unused wires.
ERROR: Assert `port_declared == true' failed in kernel/rtlil.cc:1352.
```

Note that `\B` above is actually a port, making that statement false.

Bonus points: Running `yosys` in the way above without the `-g` argument hides the error outright, i.e.:
```
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\_90_lut'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

2.2. Continuing TECHMAP pass.
$
```
